title: Holistic space observation: the shift from SSA to SDA
summary: SpaceX's proposal to deploy up to one million satellites in low Earth orbit, combined with AI-enabled autonomous orbital infrastructure, signals a major shift toward large-scale, autonomous space operations. This move highlights the transition from Space Situational Awareness (SSA) to Space Domain Awareness (SDA) in the space industry.

title: ESA to fly dedicated Crew Dragon mission to ISS
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will charter a SpaceX Crew Dragon mission to the International Space Station (ISS) to provide additional flight opportunities for its astronauts. This marks a significant collaboration between ESA and SpaceX for crewed missions.

title: Kayhan targets investors, insurers with expanded orbital intelligence platform
summary: Kayhan Space is expanding its orbital intelligence platform to offer business insights for investors and insurers. The new software leverages existing satellite fleet coordination data for broader commercial applications.

title: Blue Origin joins the orbital data center race
summary: Blue Origin has entered the orbital data center market by proposing a constellation of up to 51,600 satellites. This move positions the company as a competitor in the emerging space-based data infrastructure sector.

title: Register now: The energy imperative driving the push toward orbital data centers
summary: A virtual event on March 31, sponsored by Star Catcher and the Commercial Space Federation, will discuss the push toward orbital data centers driven by energy imperatives. The event highlights the growing interest in space-based computing infrastructure.

title: Kratos wins $446 million Space Force contract for missile-tracking ground systems
summary: Kratos secured a $446 million contract from the U.S. Space Force to develop ground management and integration systems for the Resilient Missile Warning and Tracking satellite program. This deal highlights the growing focus on missile defense and space-based tracking capabilities.

title: Portal Space Systems and Paladin Space plan debris removal service
summary: Portal Space Systems is partnering with Australian startup Paladin Space to develop a commercial orbital debris removal service using maneuverable spacecraft. This collaboration aims to address the growing issue of space debris in Earth's orbit.

title: Eileen Collins on what it takes to become Space Shuttle Commander
summary: Eileen Collins, the first woman to pilot the Space Shuttle, discusses her journey and the qualities required to become a Space Shuttle Commander in a Space Minds podcast episode. The interview highlights her career as a retired NASA astronaut and Air Force colonel.

title: NASA considering sharp increase in robotic lunar landings
summary: NASA is considering a significant increase in the frequency of robotic lunar lander missions, which has sparked both excitement and questions within the space community.

title: ReOrbit sells two small GEO satellites to SLI
summary: ReOrbit has sold two small geostationary orbit communications satellites to SLI under a new contract. This deal marks a significant transaction in the small GEO satellite market.

title: NASA's Artemis 2 moon rocket arrives back at the launch pad
summary: NASA's Space Launch System (SLS) rocket has returned to the launch pad in preparation for the Artemis 2 mission, which aims to send astronauts around the moon next month.

title: NASA won't give up hope on silent MAVEN Mars probe: 'We're still looking for it'
summary: NASA continues efforts to re-establish contact with its MAVEN Mars orbiter after a planned communications blackout ended on January 16. The agency remains optimistic about restoring communication with the silent probe.

title: Astronomers missed a space explosion as powerful as a billion suns — until they spotted its echo
summary: Astronomers missed a gamma-ray burst with energy equivalent to a billion suns until detecting its echo. The cosmic explosion was initially unnoticed.

title: A not-so-equal equinox: Why day beats night on the 1st day of spring
summary: The spring equinox marks the first day of spring, but daylight lasts several minutes longer than night due to atmospheric refraction and the size of the Sun's disk.

title: These cotton candy exoplanets hide behind a haze even the James Webb Space Telescope can't penetrate
summary: Scientists are struggling to study the atmospheres of ultra-low-density exoplanets due to a thick haze that blocks observations, even from the James Webb Space Telescope. These 'cotton candy' worlds remain shrouded in mystery.

title: 'A great relief!' Europe's Proba-3 solar-eclipse satellite phones home after a month of silence
summary: Europe's Proba-3 solar-eclipse satellite reestablished contact after a month of silence. The mission consists of two spacecraft designed to study the solar corona.

title: Earthshine will reveal the moon's hidden face tonight— here's how to see it
summary: A phenomenon called earthshine will illuminate the moon's hidden face tonight, making it visible as a young crescent moon low after sunset.

title: Private South Korean rocket exploded last December due to hardware failure, investigation finds
summary: South Korean startup Innospace's first orbital launch attempt failed in December due to a hardware failure, resulting in the explosion of its private rocket during the mission that carried five satellites.

title: Lego's first-ever Tintin set is coming next month and it's a space-themed Lego Tintin Moon rocket
summary: Lego is releasing a new space-themed Tintin Moon Rocket set featuring six minifigures on April 1. The set combines Lego, Tintin, and space themes, appealing to fans of all three.

title: NASA Simulations Improve Artemis II Launch Environment
summary: NASA used advanced simulations to optimize airflow around the Artemis II Space Launch System (SLS) rocket during its test flight around the Moon. Engineers utilized a NASA-developed tool called the Launch, Ascent, and Vehicle Aerodynamics (LAVA) to improve launch conditions.

title: NASA Glenn Opens Applications for Free Summer Engineering Institute
summary: NASA Glenn Research Center is accepting applications for its free 2026 High School Engineering Institute, a summer program aimed at preparing high school juniors and seniors for aerospace careers. Applications open March 20 and close May 1.

title: Restless Kīlauea Launches Lava and Ash
summary: Kīlauea volcano in Hawaii entered its 43rd eruption phase, producing high lava fountains and spreading ash over a wide area. The event highlights ongoing volcanic activity in the region.

title: American Bald Eagle at NASA’s Kennedy Space Center
summary: NASA's Kennedy Space Center in Florida conducted annual bald eagle nesting surveys on March 13, 2026, documenting active and inactive nests. An American bald eagle was observed flying away from its nest during the survey.

title: How NASA is Collecting Explosion Data for Next Generation Rockets
summary: NASA is gathering explosion data to enhance safety for next-generation rockets, particularly those using liquid oxygen and methane propulsion. Engineers with extensive cryogenic and test operations experience are leading this effort as rocket systems scale up.

title: NASA Laser Reflecting Instrument Makes GPS Satellite More Accurate
summary: NASA's laser reflecting technology, launched aboard GPS III SV-09, is now operational to enhance GPS accuracy. The instrument, called a laser retroreflector array (LRA), was deployed on January 27 as part of the U.S. Space Force's Block III GPS satellites.

title: NASA to Cover Progress 94 Spacecraft Launch, Space Station Docking
summary: NASA will broadcast the launch and docking of Roscosmos' Progress 94 cargo spacecraft, which is set to deliver supplies to the International Space Station on March 22. The unpiloted spacecraft will carry food, fuel, and other essentials for the ISS crew.

title: Meet the Platypi: NASA’s Newest Astronaut Candidate Class
summary: NASA's newest astronaut candidate class, named the Platypi, is training to become flight-eligible for future missions to the ISS, Moon, and Mars. The 2025-selected candidates are developing technical and operational skills at Johnson Space Center.

title: NASA’s Roman Observatory Passes Final Major Prelaunch Tests
summary: NASA's Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope has successfully completed final prelaunch tests, including extreme sound exposure, shaking, and electronic checks. The observatory is now on track for its upcoming launch.

title: Australia’s “Red Centre” Turns Green
summary: Australia's Central Australia, known as the 'Red Centre,' experienced a significant transformation in early 2026 due to heavy rainfall in February and March. The desert landscape turned green, marking an unusual environmental change.

title: Booster 19 concludes initial test campaign on Pad 2
summary: Booster 19 has completed its initial test campaign on Pad 2, marking another step forward for SpaceX's Starship Flight 12 mission.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9, Spectrum, Electron to launch during busy week of launches
summary: A busy launch week is scheduled with nine launches planned, including missions by SpaceX's Falcon 9, Rocket Lab's Electron, and Isar Aerospace's Spectrum.

title: Blue Origin Unveils NEO Hunter: A Hybrid Planetary Defense Concept
summary: Blue Origin introduced NEO Hunter, a hybrid planetary defense concept aimed at protecting Earth from near-Earth objects. The mission concept highlights Blue Origin's involvement in space safety and defense technologies.

title: From Steel Rolls to Starship at the Starfactory
summary: SpaceX is accelerating its plans for human missions to the Moon and Mars at its Starfactory facility, transitioning from steel production to spacecraft manufacturing. The company is expanding its infrastructure to support ambitious space exploration goals.

title: New crew arrivals and science highlight February aboard the ISS
summary: A new crew arrived at the International Space Station (ISS) in February following the early departure of Crew-11 in January. The month was marked by new crew arrivals and scientific activities aboard the ISS.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9 to dominate another busy week of missions
summary: Eight launches are scheduled worldwide this week, with SpaceX's Falcon 9 rocket dominating the launch schedule. The upcoming missions highlight Falcon 9's continued dominance in the space industry.

title: SpaceX’s Enters a new era with Booster 19 Testing on Pad 2
summary: SpaceX has begun testing Booster 19 on Pad 2, marking a new phase since Flight 11 in October 2025. This follows a 147-day gap since the last mission.

title: Firefly Alpha FLTA007 “Stairway to Seven” launches successfully
summary: Firefly Aerospace successfully launched its Firefly Alpha FLTA007 'Stairway to Seven' rocket, marking its return to flight after a nearly 10-month pause. The mission represents a significant milestone for the company's space launch capabilities.

title: Baikonur Launch Pad restored, clearing path for Progress MS-33 ISS Mission
summary: Russia's Baikonur Cosmodrome’s Site 31/6 launch pad has been fully restored, enabling the upcoming Progress MS-33 mission to the International Space Station (ISS). The repair work clears the path for the critical resupply mission.

title: NASA’s Artemis ‘Course Correction’ Boosts Moonward Momentum: Key Details Still Maturing
summary: NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman announced significant changes to the Artemis program to restore its momentum, though key details are still being developed.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: The TEC Business to Business website has reported on industry developments without specific details provided in the given text.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) released a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over its 50-year history. The publication celebrates the agency's milestones and contributions to space exploration.

title: Proba-3’s Coronagraph is alive!
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft has re-established contact with ground control after a month-long anomaly. The mission team confirmed the spacecraft is operational and has 'phoned home'.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is hosting events to present the follow-up to the Council Meeting at Ministerial Level 2025 (CM25), detailing planned activities, priorities, and industrial opportunities. These events aim to facilitate direct exchange between ESA and the industrial community.

title: Plato readies for space-like tests
summary: ESA's Plato mission, designed to discover Earth-like exoplanets, is undergoing space-like tests in the Large Space Simulator (LSS) chamber. The spacecraft, equipped with 26 ultrasensitive cameras, is expected to launch on an Ariane 6 rocket in January 2027.

title: Former NASA astronaut Jeanette Epps visits ESTEC
summary: Former NASA astronaut Jeanette Epps visited ESTEC, the European Space Agency's technical heart. This visit highlights international collaboration in space exploration.

title: ESA rolls out new costing software for its suppliers
summary: ESA will release ECOS version 6 on 30 March 2026, requiring all suppliers to use the new software for financial proposals in ESA Tender Actions.

title: Work ongoing to restore contact with Proba-3's Coronagraph
summary: An anomaly onboard the Proba-3 mission’s Coronagraph spacecraft caused a loss of contact with ground control. Mission teams are investigating the issue and working to restore communication.

title: Students kick off experiments on ESA’s flat floor
summary: Students have initiated experiments on ESA's flat floor facility, which simulates low-gravity environments for testing robotic systems. This initiative aims to advance space technology research and education.

title: Kayhan Space Launches Satcat Terminal
summary: Kayhan Space has launched a new business called Satcat Terminal. This development is expected to benefit liberal arts majors.

title: Inside Austria’s Small But Mighty Space Ecosystem
summary: Austria's space industry is experiencing growth with increased funding and startup activity. The country is emerging as a significant player in the space sector despite its smaller scale.

title: Portal, Paladin Team On Debris Removal Service
summary: Portal and Paladin are collaborating on a debris removal service aimed at making debris management operational rather than experimental. The initiative emphasizes the importance of satellite data for critical infrastructure like communications, navigation, weather forecasting, and national security.

title: HyImpulse Signs Launch Agreement with SaxaVord
summary: HyImpulse has signed a launch agreement with SaxaVord for its second suborbital flight of the SR75 rocket, scheduled for no earlier than Q3 2026.

title: Apex Signs First Japanese Bus Contract With NEC
summary: Apex has signed its first contract in Japan with NEC Corporation to purchase an Aries satellite bus for a communications demonstration mission scheduled for next year. This marks Apex's entry into the Japanese market.

title: Modified Vulcan Expected to Launch This Summer
summary: The Vulcan rocket is set to launch this summer with modifications to improve its nozzle and solid rocket booster margins. Changes were accelerated following an anomaly to ensure a successful launch.

title: Advanced Navigation Raises $110M Series C
summary: Advanced Navigation, an Australia-based company, secured $110 million in a Series C funding round. The company aims to reduce global reliance on satellite infrastructure.

title: NanoAvionics Lands Korean National Security Deal
summary: NanoAvionics has secured a deal with South Korea's national security sector, marking another success in its global expansion strategy. The agreement highlights the growing collaboration between NanoAvionics and the South Korean space industry.

title: FAA Ends Part 450 Transition Period
summary: The FAA has concluded the Part 450 transition period, requiring all US launchers to comply with updated five-year-old licensing regulations. No operators remain under the legacy system.

title: How Congress Can Grow the Space Supply Chain
summary: The space industry's supply chain is struggling to meet growing demand, but policymakers can accelerate growth according to two major industry groups.

title: Spire Targets 50% Growth in 2026 After Adjustments in 2025
summary: Spire Global aims for a 50% revenue growth in 2026 following strategic adjustments in 2025, including divesting its maritime business and reducing debt. CEO Theresa Condor outlined this ambitious target to investors.

title: Eutelsat CEO Fallacher Talks OneWeb Gen 2, Global Competition, and Sovereign Demand
summary: Eutelsat Group CEO Jean-François Fallacher discusses the company's strategic initiatives, including OneWeb Gen 2, global competition, and sovereign demand, during his nearly one-year tenure.

title: Portal Space Systems and Paladin Space Plan Repeatable Debris Removal Missions
summary: Portal Space Systems and Paladin Space are collaborating to provide commercial debris removal missions under a recurring service model called Debris Removal as a Service (DRAAS). This partnership aims to address the growing issue of space debris through regular, commercialized cleanup operations.
company: Portal Space Systems and Paladin Space

title: SLI Purchases Two Small GEO Satellites From ReOrbit
summary: SLI is set to purchase two small Geostationary Orbit (GEO) communication satellites from ReOrbit for 150 million euros ($172.8 million). The deal was announced in a joint statement by both companies.

title: Comtech Delivers First Set of Digital Modems to US Army
summary: Comtech delivered the first set of Enterprise Digital Intermediate Frequency Multi-Carrier (EDIM) modems to the U.S. Army under a $48.6 million contract signed in 2023. The company announced the delivery in a recent press release.

title: Latest White House Cybersecurity Strategy Talks Crypto, AI, Quantum
summary: The White House released President Trump’s Cyber Strategy for America, focusing on securing critical infrastructure and emerging technologies like AI and quantum computing. The strategy outlines six policy pillars to maintain technological superiority.

title: Getting Better Outcomes In Space: How Will the UK Embrace the Challenge?
summary: A panel at Space-Comm in London discussed the security of UK space assets and strategic stability from government and industry perspectives.

title: SpiderOak Wins New UAS Systems Contract
summary: SpiderOak has secured a new contract with the U.S. Army Unmanned Aircraft Systems (PMO UAS) to conduct supply chain analysis and cybersecurity assessments for multiple vendors. The contract focuses on enhancing security and reliability in UAS systems.

title: Ukraine Ambassador to UK Talks Future Warfare
summary: The Ukrainian ambassador to the UK discussed the future of warfare at a Chatham House event, four years after Russia's invasion. The speech focused on evolving military strategies and technologies.

title: Ligado Tells FCC That SkyTerra Next Proposal Won’t Cause Interference
summary: Ligado has filed a proposal with the FCC to host an L-band payload on AST SpaceMobile satellites, asserting that it will not cause interference. The company claims the plan aligns with existing L-band coordination agreements.
